mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS rises >100k and UNRATE falls >0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate improving demand conditions that support equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS exceed their 3-month highs.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when INDPRO records consecutive monthly increases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation below 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals stable price expectations that reduce equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 40%+ of periods where CPIAUCSL remains below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions that lower corporate earnings forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding economic activity that raises corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when GDPC1 records consecutive quarterly growth.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates favorable monetary conditions that drive equity market gains over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 40%+ of instances where both FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL < 2%.
